label: Deltoid contusion without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a deltoid bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a deltoid bruise. We did not find fracture, infection, open wound, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue fingers, pale fingers, or severe worsening pain.
New deformity, inability to use the area, fever, spreading redness, or drainage.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated deltoid contusion.
No fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care, simple comfort measures, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, infection signs, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed deltoid contusion with fracture, wound, infection, neurovascular, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of deltoid contusion

label: Clavicle contusion without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a clavicle bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a clavicle bruise. We did not find fracture, infection, open wound, or poor blood flow today.
home: Rest the shoulder area as needed.
Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue fingers, pale fingers, or severe worsening pain.

No fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care, simple comfort measures, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, infection signs, or concerning symptoms. label: Biceps strain without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a biceps strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a biceps strain. We did not find fracture, tendon rupture, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Rest the arm from activity that makes pain worse.
Use ice, heat, gentle motion, support, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue or pale fingers, or severe worsening pain.
A pop, new deformity, inability to use the arm, fever, spreading redness, or drainage.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, physical therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated biceps strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture concern, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture concern, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, inability to use the limb, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus sprain and strain material supports rest, activity modification, symptom care, gradual return, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, or inability to move. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed biceps strain with rupture, fracture, wound, infection, neurovascular, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of biceps strain
